title: 10 best herbal essential oils for grey hair
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for grey hair are a natural and effective way to promote healthy hair growth, improve texture, and reduce graying. These oils work by nourishing the scalp, stimulating blood flow, and neutralizing free radicals that can cause hair to turn grey. Examples of herbal essential oils that help with grey hair include rosemary, sage, and thyme, which can be used in a blend or individually. Using these oils has been shown to improve lives by giving individuals a sense of confidence and youthfulness, as well as reducing stress and anxiety related to premature graying.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree essential oils helps with grey hair because of its remarkable antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as cineole and terpinen-4-ol, have been shown to stimulate hair growth, improve scalp circulation, and reduce oxidative stress that can contribute to graying. Additionally, tea tree oil's antifungal and antibacterial properties can help prevent dandruff and other scalp conditions that may accelerate hair whitening. By using a shampoo or conditioner containing tea tree essential oils, individuals can potentially slow down the graying process and promote healthier-looking locks.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Tea Tree Oil has antioxidant properties that can help protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to premature graying.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the scalp, which may be associated with grey hair.
Stimulates blood circulation: Tea Tree Oil can stimulate blood circulation to the scalp, which can promote healthy hair growth and potentially slow down or even reverse grey hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and antimicrobial compounds, tea tree roots help combat fungal infections that can cause premature graying.
Leaves: Abundant in flavonoids and terpenes, tea tree leaves promote healthy scalp circulation, which can improve hair growth and reduce gray hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves at dawn when dew is still present on the foliage.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with filtered water to remove dirt and impurities gently.
Steam-distill the washed leaves in a copper or stainless steel alembic for 4-6 hours patiently.
Monitor and record the distillation process closely as essential oil yields peak at optimal temperatures carefully.
Filter and bottle the resulting melaleuca alternifolia essential oil immediately after distillation with precision.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with grey hair because of its remarkable ability to stimulate blood flow and circulation to the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth. The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties in rosemary oil also help to combat oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to premature graying of hair. Additionally, rosemary oil's natural astringent properties help to strengthen hair follicles, leading to thicker, darker, and shinier locks. Regular use of rosemary essential oils has been shown to promote a natural pigmentation process, resulting in reduced grey hair and a more youthful appearance.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Rosemary essential oil's antioxidant properties help protect the hair follicles from oxidative stress, which can cause premature graying and hair loss, thus promoting healthy hair growth.

Improves Circulation: Rosemary essential oil's stimulant properties improve blood circulation to the scalp, which helps to nourish and oxygenate the hair follicles, reducing grey hair and promoting a healthy, natural hair color.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Rosemary essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can contribute to grey hair and hair loss, thus promoting a healthier scalp environment for hair growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots have been traditionally used in aromatherapy to stimulate blood circulation and promote healthy hair growth, making them an effective choice for combating grey hair.
Leaves: Rosemary leaves contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation on the scalp, contributing to premature graying of hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosemary leaves at dawn or late evening to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested rosemary leaves in a warm dry place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture.
Steam distill the dried rosemary leaves using a distillation apparatus and 300ml of water for 60 minutes.
Filter the distilled essential oil through a coffee filter paper or cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle.
Store the filtered rosmarinus officinalis essential oil in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with grey hair because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that promote healthy scalp and hair follicles. The oil's antifungal and antibacterial properties also help to combat dandruff, itchiness, and other scalp issues that can contribute to premature graying. Additionally, thyme's stimulating properties improve blood circulation, which nourishes the hair roots and encourages natural pigmentation. As a result, using thyme essential oils can help to reverse or slow down the graying process, promoting thicker, healthier, and more vibrant-looking hair.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant property: Thyme essential oil's antioxidant property helps protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to premature greying.
Stimulant property: The stimulant property of thyme essential oil helps increase blood circulation to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and potentially reducing grey hair.

Astringent property: Thyme essential oil's astringent property helps to constrict the pores on the scalp, which can help reduce inflammation and promote a healthy environment for hair growth, potentially slowing down or reversing grey hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in promoting hair growth and reducing grey hair.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain flavonoids and terpenes, which help to nourish the scalp, promote hair health, and reduce inflammation that can contribute to grey hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves at dawn when they are most potent and rich in bioactive compounds.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 8 hours or until completely dry.
Grind 100g of dried thymus vulgaris leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Steam distill the powdered thymus vulgaris in a still apparatus with 500ml of distilled water at 100°c for 2 hours to extract the essential oil.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter paper and store it in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid, ready for use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with grey hair because of its natural ability to promote healthy hair growth, reduce oxidative stress, and improve circulation. The oil's antifungal and antibacterial properties also help to soothe an itchy scalp, which can contribute to premature graying. Furthermore, the calming and relaxing properties of English lavender can help to reduce stress and anxiety, known triggers for grey hair. By incorporating English lavender essential oils into your hair care routine, you may be able to slow down or even reverse the greying process, promoting a fuller and healthier head of hair.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: English lavender essential oil's antioxidant properties help to protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to premature greying and thinning.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory properties of English lavender essential oil may help to reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can contribute to grey hair and promote healthy hair growth.
Antimicrobial properties: English lavender essential oil's antimicrobial properties may help to combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause scalp irritation and accelerate grey hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and saponins, which can help promote hair growth and reduce graying.
Flowers: Lavender flowers contain flavonoids and terpenes that can improve scalp circulation and promote healthy hair follicles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in quantities of 100 grams at optimal blooming time for maximum oil yield.
Dry the harvested flowers by spreading them out on paper towels and allowing air circulation for a minimum of 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried lavandula angustifolia flowers using 100 milliliters of water per 50 grams of material for a duration of two hours.
Collect and filter the essential oil obtained during distillation to remove impurities and separate it from the hydrosol, yielding approximately 3-5% oil concentration.
Transfer the extracted lavandula angustifolia essential oil into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store them in a cool, dry place for up to six months while maintaining its potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sandalwood essential oils helps with grey hair because it promotes healthy scalp circulation, nourishes the follicles, and stimulates melanin production. The sesquiterpenes present in sandalwood oil also help to regulate hormonal imbalances that can contribute to premature graying. Additionally, sandalwood's antioxidant properties protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, which can accelerate the greying process. By using sandalwood essential oils regularly, individuals may experience a reduction in grey hair and an overall improvement in their hair's health and appearance.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sandalwood essential oil's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress, which contributes to premature graying of hair.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sandalwood essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can lead to hair loss and graying by damaging hair follicles.
Stress-relieving properties: Sandalwood essential oil's calming and grounding effects help to reduce stress, which is a known contributor to premature graying of hair by increasing cortisol levels.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, sandalwood roots help promote healthy hair growth and reduce premature graying.
Buds: The delicate buds contain high levels of sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to stimulate hair follicles and promote a natural color pigmentation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality santalum album leaves from organic farms or your own garden at dawn on dry days.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator set at 60°c for 12 hours or air-drying for 7-10 days.
Steam-distill 100g of dried santalum album leaves with 500ml of water in a stainless steel still for 4 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil through cheesecloth and store it in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature.
Use the freshly extracted santalum album essential oil within 6 months, applying 2-3 drops to the scalp twice daily for 3 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with grey hair because it contains antioxidants and carotenoids that nourish and protect the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth. The oil's anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce stress, which is a known contributor to premature graying. Additionally, pot marigold oil's ability to balance skin tone and improve circulation can help to distribute melanin more evenly throughout the hair shaft, reducing gray tones and promoting a healthier, more vibrant color.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: It helps to combat oxidative stress and free radical damage, which can contribute to premature graying of hair.
Anti-inflammatory: It reduces inflammation in the scalp, which can lead to hair loss and grey hair, promoting healthy hair growth and color.
Stimulates Hair Growth: The essential oil contains compounds that stimulate blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and preventing grey hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in promoting healthy hair growth and reducing the appearance of grey hairs.
Flowers: Pot marigold flowers contain flavonoids and carotenoids that help to nourish and protect the scalp, promoting a natural color and shine to the hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers and dry them thoroughly in a warm dark place for several days.
Use a high-quality steam distillation apparatus to extract the essential oil from the dried calendula flowers at a temperature of 100°c.
Mix the distilled essential oil with carrier oils such as coconut or olive oil in a ratio of 1:4 and store it in amber glass bottles for up to six months.
Use a dropper bottle to apply the hair treatment blend three times a week to the scalp massaging gently for five minutes each time.
Perform a gentle shampoo using a mild sulfate-free cleanser followed by a hydrating conditioner after applying the calendula essential oil hair treatment for optimal results within four weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe essential oils helps with grey hair because it nourishes and conditions the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth. The oil's antioxidants and vitamins B and E help to stimulate blood flow to the scalp, reducing inflammation that can contribute to premature graying. Additionally, aloe's natural anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the scalp, creating an ideal environment for new hair growth and color pigmentation to thrive.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Herbal aloe essential oil's antioxidant properties help protect the hair follicles from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to premature graying.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory properties of aloe vera essential oil reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can slow down or even reverse grey hair growth, promoting healthy and vibrant hair.
Stimulation of Hair Growth: Herbal aloe essential oil stimulates blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing the appearance of grey hair by encouraging melanin production.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their high concentration of aloin, a natural dye that helps to stimulate hair growth and promote a healthy scalp.
Roots: Used to create an anti-inflammatory oil that soothes the scalp and reduces irritation, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves and extract gel using a juicer or blender.
Combine extracted aloe vera gel with rosemary essential oil in a 3:1 ratio by volume.
Mix in henna powder in small amounts until a smooth paste forms taking about 10 minutes.
Leave the mixture to infuse for at least 24 hours allowing natural processes to unfold.
Strain and filter the mixture using cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with grey hair because of its potent antioxidant properties. The oil's high concentration of eucalyptol and other bioactive compounds helps to stimulate blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing oxidative stress that can contribute to premature graying. Additionally, the oil's antifungal and antibacterial properties may help to remove impurities and product buildup from the hair and scalp, allowing for a healthy environment for hair follicles to thrive.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil has been shown to have high antioxidant activity, which can help combat oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, potentially slowing down or reversing the greying process.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the scalp, which is often associated with hair loss and graying, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth and color retention.

Cytotoxic properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil has been found to exhibit cytotoxic activity against certain cancer cells, including those that contribute to grey hair, suggesting its potential in preventing or reversing the formation of grey hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian Blue Gum contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in combating premature graying and promoting healthy hair growth.
Barks: The barks of the Tasmanian Blue Gum have been traditionally used to treat various scalp conditions, including dandruff and itchiness, which can contribute to grey hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in early morning or late afternoon for optimal oil content.
Rinse the harvested leaves thoroughly with filtered water to remove dirt and impurities gently.
Steam distill the washed leaves using a still apparatus at 100°c temperature for 30-40 minutes patiently.
Combine the resulting essential oil with carrier oils such as coconut or olive oil in a 1:10 ratio carefully.
Store the final blended oil in dark glass bottles away from direct sunlight and heat sources neatly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange essential oils helps with grey hair because of its rich antioxidant properties, which help to stimulate melanin production in the hair follicles. As a result, it promotes natural hair pigmentation and can help to slow down the graying process. Additionally, bitter orange oil's stimulating effect on blood circulation can also enhance overall scalp health, leading to thicker, fuller, and more vibrant-looking locks. By using herbal bitter orange essential oils as a natural treatment, individuals can potentially reverse or reduce grey hair and enjoy a more youthful and radiant appearance.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Bergamot essential oil helps protect the hair follicles from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to premature graying.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Bergamot essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation in the scalp, which is thought to be a contributing factor to grey hair.

Stress-relieving properties: Bergamot essential oil has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate stress and anxiety, both of which are linked to hair graying.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high content of flavonoids and limonoids, which have been shown to promote hair growth and reduce graying.
Barks: The barks contain a higher concentration of limonoids, particularly limonin, which has antioxidant properties that help protect the scalp and prevent premature greying.
Seeds: The seeds are rich in fatty acids and contain compounds like naringenin, which have been shown to stimulate hair growth and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh citrus peel from organic oranges or mandarins in the morning.
Dry the harvested citrus peel for at least 24 hours to reduce moisture content.
Use a cold-pressing method to extract the essential oil from the dried citrus peel within 2 weeks.
Mix the extracted essential oil with 10% jojoba oil and store it in dark glass bottles at room temperature for 30 days.
Filter the mixture through a coffee filter paper or cheesecloth and discard any solids before using the herbal citrus x aurantium essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with grey hair because it promotes a healthy scalp and follicles, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to premature graying. The antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of chamomile oil help to nourish the hair roots, improving circulation and promoting a natural pigmentation process. Additionally, chamomile oil's ability to soothe the skin may also help to reduce stress-induced hair whitening, leading to a healthier, more vibrant mane.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Chamomile oil helps combat oxidative stress, which can contribute to grey hair by neutralizing free radicals that damage hair follicles and cause pigmentation loss.
Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help soothe irritated scalps and reduce inflammation that can lead to premature graying of hair.
Stress-relieving: Chamomile oil's calming and relaxing effects can help manage stress, which is a known contributor to grey hair, by promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels in the body.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Chamomile flowers are a popular choice for making essential oils due to their soothing and calming properties, which can help reduce stress and promote healthy hair growth.
Roots: Chamomile roots have been traditionally used to treat various scalp issues, including dandruff and itchiness, making them beneficial for grey hair care.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried flowers in a still at atmospheric pressure for 2-3 hours yielding 20ml of oil.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or paper towel to remove any impurities and sediment.
Store the filtered oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool place for up to 6 months.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat grey hair with herbal essential oils is by using a blend of lavender, rosemary, and chamomile oils. Massage a few drops of this mixture into your scalp to stimulate blood flow and promote healthy hair growth. Additionally, add a few drops of bergamot oil to the mix for its uplifting properties and to help balance the natural pigmentation of your hair. Regular use can lead to a vibrant and healthy-looking mane.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to grey hair that are treated with herbal essential oils are premature aging, wrinkles, and skin dryness. Rosemary essential oil is known to promote healthy hair growth and improve circulation, while Geranium essential oil helps to balance hormones and reduce stress. Frankincense essential oil also aids in reducing fine lines and wrinkles, while Lavender oil soothes and moisturizes the skin. These oils can be used individually or blended together for a holistic approach to addressing these age-related concerns.
